channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Nasdaq falls 4% and suffers worst day since April 2025 as traders flee chip stocks: Live updates
item.description: All three major averages closed out a losing week.

item.title: Meta's stock sinks on report company could raise tens of billions of dollars to fund AI push
item.description: Meta shares dropped after the Financial Times reported the company could potentially raise tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to help its AI push.

item.title: Google to pay SpaceX $920 million a month for compute capacity at xAI data centers
item.description: Ahead of a planned IPO, SpaceX inked a deal to rent compute capacity to Google for $920 million per month for 32 months.

item.title: Bitcoin cracks $60,000, sinking to lowest level since October 2024
item.description: The weeklong sell-off was exacerbated after a stronger-than-expected May jobs report Friday sent yields higher and pressured risk assets.

item.title: SpaceX is set to be a 'seminal event' for the stock market next week. Here's what's ahead
item.description: What the first of several trillion-dollar companies going public will mean for a stock market that's already at all-time highs.

item.title: Marvell Technology and Flex to join S&P 500 index, replacing Pool and Campbell's
item.description: The move highlights the growing importance of the technology sector to the stock market.

item.title: A sell-off like Friday's is ‘rarely the top’ of a rally, says Wharton’s Jeremy Siegel
item.description: The artificial intelligence-driven rally is different from previous market bubbles, according to Siegel, who likened it to the Industrial Revolution.

item.title: Mythos rejuvenated the cybersecurity sector. Earnings put the recent rally to the test
item.description: Mythos gave the cybersecurity sector a boost, but upbeat earnings weren't enough for investors in search of an AI payoff.

item.title: Iran's threats against this Red Sea choke point are a big vulnerability for the oil market
item.description: Oil prices would spike if Iran's Houthi allies started attacking ships passing through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

item.title: Crypto mutiny on Strategy: Shorts target 'MSTR' in bitcoin bloodbath
item.description: Flows around Strategy (MSTR) and the company's variable-rate preferred stock STRC are turning bearish this week.

item.title: Warren Buffett disciple Guy Spier built career on value investing. A rare cancer changed everything
item.description: After a glioblastoma diagnosis, value investor Guy Spier rethought wealth, time and legacy — and the impact he can have on rare disease research.

item.title: Inside the $850 million Obama Presidential Center ahead of its Juneteenth opening
item.description: President Obama's library will cost $30 to visit, making it the most expensive presidential museum or library in modern history.

item.title: Trump wants Bill Pulte to fire big chunk of national intelligence office staff: WSJ
item.description: President Trump's comments are sure to roil members of Congress who criticized the appointment of Bill Pulte as acting national intelligence director.

item.title: Chip stock breakout vs the rest of tech showing signs of topping out, says Carter Worth
item.description: The Philadelphia Stock Exchange Semiconductor Index finally has recouped all its relative losses to the S&P 500 Information Technology Sector.

item.title: Trump administration, OpenAI discussing possible government stake in the AI startup
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman first shared the idea with the Trump administration in 2025, according to a source.

item.title: Hot jobs report puts Fed cuts further out of reach as Chair Warsh faces policy tests
item.description: Another big jobs report in May has swept aside the possibility of interest rate cuts anytime soon.

item.title: Alphabet is seeking fresh capital as stock's 4-week losing streak tests investor appetite
item.description: Alphabet expects capex to reach up to $190 billion this year, double last year's spending, and the company is turning to investors to help fund its expansion.

item.title: Prediction market firms face growing scrutiny as Congress weighs regulation
item.description: Rep. Bryan Steil, R-Wisc., is working on a bill to ban Congress members and their staff from certain bets on prediction markets such as Kalshi and Polymarket.

item.title: Delta SkyMiles credit cards now come with 2 free checked bags and monster intro offers. Are they worth it?
item.description: From two free checked bags to new rideshare credits, Delta SkyMiles cards now offer hundreds of dollars in added value each year — for the same annual fees.

item.title: Apple's WWDC: Tim Cook's AI legacy at stake in his final developer conference as CEO
item.description: Apple heads into WWDC with Tim Cook’s AI legacy, Siri’s future as an agentic platform, and the stock’s rich valuation all on the line.

item.title: As AI-related stocks dive, the market's winners have one thing in common
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Model routing is a fix for AI overspending. That's a problem for OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: Companies are shifting from running everything on the most powerful AI model to matching each task to the right one, a practice called model routing.

item.title: U.S. Senate passes $70 billion ICE funding, fails to ban Trump's 'anti-weaponization' fund
item.description: Nearly all of the funding would go to DHS's ICE and Border Patrol agencies that are carrying out the Trump admin's vigorous deportations throughout the U.S.

item.title: Boeing to start 737 Max production on new assembly line July 6, CEO says
item.description: The new 737 Max final assembly line in Everett, Washington, will serve as a catalyst for increasing Max production to 52 jets per month.

item.title: Lululemon cuts annual outlook, citing 'negative' media commentary and disappointing product launches
item.description: Lululemon is expecting its situation to get a lot worse before it gets better, as it issued weak guidance for the full year.

item.title: The gold chart looks precarious. Here's how to profit
item.description: Gold is at a technically precarious juncture, and the good news for you is that options market may be mispricing the risk.

item.title: No tech, no problem. Dow makes record and traders think these stocks can lead now
item.description: Traders bought about 5,300 calls in the State Street Health Care Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLV) on Thursday, compared to just over 1,000 puts, according to data from ThinkOrSwim.

item.title: Odds of a Fed hike this year jump on prediction markets
item.description: Following an unexpectedly hot jobs report, prediction market traders see a 52% chance the Federal Reserve increases interest rates this year.

item.title: AI is designing OpenAI's next model in a sign of 'superintelligence': SoftBank's Masayoshi Son to CNBC
item.description: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son said his forecast of artificial superintelligence arriving in 10 years was "conservative" and thinks it will be here sooner.

item.title: Amazon unveils latest warehouse robot as tech giants continue AI layoffs
item.description: "Our experience of robots is that it's actually driven up employment rather than the reverse," Amazon executive John Boumphrey told CNBC.
